"verify-ui-beautiful": The dev server has been started

Used the active verify-ui-beautiful trajectory and self-started the dashboard locally because localhost:3101 was not listening.: Used the active verify-ui-beautiful trajectory and self-started the dashboard locally because localhost:3101 was not listening.

raw.question: Used the active verify-ui-beautiful trajectory and self-started the dashboard locally because localhost:3101 was not listening.
raw.chosen: Used the active verify-ui-beautiful trajectory and self-started the dashboard locally because localhost:3101 was not listening.
raw.reasoning: The verification target must be reachable for browser inspection, and the current workflow chapter already tracks this task.

events.content: Add direct tree/read CLI commands: Add direct tree/read CLI commands

raw.question: Add direct tree/read CLI commands
raw.chosen: Add direct tree/read CLI commands
raw.reasoning: Cloud test scripts exercise fs/tree and fs/file directly; existing CLI only exposes those through mount/export, so first-class commands make the same checks possible with user-provided server/token credentials.

events.content: CLI parity gap addressed with tree/read commands; next step is focused Go verification and dashboard setup validation.
Verify dashboard with package-local install: Verify dashboard with package-local install

raw.question: Verify dashboard with package-local install
raw.chosen: Verify dashboard with package-local install
raw.reasoning: The observer test suite depends on package devDependencies such as jsdom, which were not installed in the workspace; npm ci from the package lock preserves package metadata while enabling local verification.

events.content: Verification passed for CLI and observer build; only remaining setup question is whether to run the dashboard with user-supplied credentials or a config-required local shell.

Completed CLI direct tree/read parity and local observer verification. Go CLI/httpapi tests pass; observer vitest and Next build pass; dev server is available on port 3101 pending user credentials for live data.
Refine observer content and health empty states: Refine observer content and health empty states

raw.question: Refine observer content and health empty states
raw.chosen: Refine observer content and health empty states
raw.reasoning: Live cloud data showed JSON files rendered as a raw single-line string and an empty provider health response consumed excessive vertical space; both are presentation issues in the observer page, not API issues.

events.content: Observer dashboard preview fixes implemented: JSON content is formatted for application/json or .json files, and the health card no longer stretches to fill the grid row when provider status is empty.
Move workspace health into dashboard header: Move workspace health into dashboard header

raw.question: Move workspace health into dashboard header
raw.chosen: Move workspace health into dashboard header
raw.reasoning: The provider sync panel duplicates the existing header status and wastes the primary workspace area when sync/status returns no providers; making it a compact header state gives search and tree browsing more room.

events.content: Moved workspace health to the header and widened the search/tree area. Observer tests and production build passed after the layout change.
Make file details panel sticky: Make file details panel sticky

raw.question: Make file details panel sticky
raw.chosen: Make file details panel sticky
raw.reasoning: The file tree can be long, and selected-file context should remain visible while browsing; sticky behavior should apply only when the two-column desktop layout is active.

events.content: Made file details sticky on xl layouts with internal overflow. Observer tests and build passed.
Constrain observer main panels to viewport height: Constrain observer main panels to viewport height

raw.question: Constrain observer main panels to viewport height
raw.chosen: Constrain observer main panels to viewport height
raw.reasoning: Sticky details inside a page-scrolling layout can still disappear on non-xl widths or when ancestor overflow bounds the sticky area; making the file tree and details panels independent viewport-bounded scroll regions keeps details visible while browsing.

events.content: Reworked observer scrolling so desktop layout is viewport-bounded: search/tree scrolls internally and file details remains visible. Tests and build passed.
Add double-click full content modal: Add double-click full content modal

raw.question: Add double-click full content modal
raw.chosen: Add double-click full content modal
raw.reasoning: The details pane is optimized for metadata and clipped previews; users need a focused viewer for complete file content, especially JSON, without losing tree context.

events.content: Add observer write/edit path: Add observer write/edit path

raw.question: Add observer write/edit path
raw.chosen: Add observer write/edit path
raw.reasoning: Users need to edit files from the observer; RelayFile writes require optimistic concurrency via If-Match and fs:write-scoped tokens, so the UI should surface editing inside the full-view modal and refresh details after save.

events.content: Investigate Notion directory load failure at core: Investigate Notion directory load failure at core

raw.question: Investigate Notion directory load failure at core
raw.chosen: Investigate Notion directory load failure at core
raw.reasoning: The dashboard reports SQLITE_ERROR from listTree for a Notion database path, which indicates a backend path/query modeling issue rather than a UI-only error; inspect core tree listing and adapter path output before changing behavior.

events.content: Infer provider filter options from root path segments: Infer provider filter options from root path segments

raw.question: Infer provider filter options from root path segments
raw.chosen: Infer provider filter options from root path segments
raw.reasoning: Root provider directories such as /github, /notion, and /slack do not always carry provider metadata, so the dashboard must derive provider choices from path namespaces before child files are loaded.

events.content: Created writeback implementation workflow: Created writeback implementation workflow

raw.question: Created writeback implementation workflow
raw.chosen: Created writeback implementation workflow
raw.reasoning: The missing writeback path spans relayfile queue handling, cloud web credential resolution, provider adapter planners, infra env wiring, and E2E validation. A dedicated agent-relay DAG workflow with tests-first and hard verification gates is the right vehicle.

events.content: Tightened RelayFile writeback workflow to require PGlite-backed workspace integration tests: Tightened RelayFile writeback workflow to require PGlite-backed workspace integration tests

raw.question: Tightened RelayFile writeback workflow to require PGlite-backed workspace integration tests
raw.chosen: Tightened RelayFile writeback workflow to require PGlite-backed workspace integration tests
raw.reasoning: The 80-to-100 workflow skill requires DB-backed validation when behavior depends on database state; pure workspace_integrations mocks were not enough evidence for writeback bridge correctness.

events.content: Opened cloud PR for RelayFile provider writeback workflow: Opened cloud PR for RelayFile provider writeback workflow

raw.question: Opened cloud PR for RelayFile provider writeback workflow
raw.chosen: Opened cloud PR for RelayFile provider writeback workflow
raw.reasoning: The user wanted the workflow available from another computer; a clean origin/main worktree avoided unrelated local untracked files and existing branch commits.

events.content: Addressed PR feedback for RelayFile writeback workflow gates: Addressed PR feedback for RelayFile writeback workflow gates

raw.question: Addressed PR feedback for RelayFile writeback workflow gates
raw.chosen: Addressed PR feedback for RelayFile writeback workflow gates
raw.reasoning: Review comments found two workflow reliability bugs: npm install failures hidden by a tail pipeline and a secret grep that matched legitimate NANGO_SECRET_KEY references. Both were narrowed to preserve root-cause failures and avoid false-positive safety gate blocks.

events.content: Route RelayFile observer under agentrelay.com observer namespace: Route RelayFile observer under agentrelay.com observer namespace

raw.question: Route RelayFile observer under agentrelay.com observer namespace
raw.chosen: Route RelayFile observer under agentrelay.com observer namespace
raw.reasoning: The existing cloud router already owns agentrelay.com path routing. Adding /observer/file as a more-specific route lets Relaycast observer keep /observer while mounting the published RelayFile observer package separately at /observer/file.

events.content: Expose RelayFile observer from active integrations only: Expose RelayFile observer from active integrations only

raw.question: Expose RelayFile observer from active integrations only
raw.chosen: Expose RelayFile observer from active integrations only
raw.reasoning: User rejected a sidebar entry; the integrations page already owns provider state, so the observer link can be shown only when the workspace has connected provider records.

events.content: Make Slack channel access lazy and collapsible: Make Slack channel access lazy and collapsible

raw.question: Make Slack channel access lazy and collapsible
raw.chosen: Make Slack channel access lazy and collapsible
raw.reasoning: The channel picker can dominate the integrations page and does not need to fetch available Slack channels until the user expands the management section.

events.content: Cloud dashboard now routes RelayFile observer discovery through the Integrations page, and Slack channel management is collapsed/lazy by default. Typecheck and diff whitespace checks passed; ESLint is blocked by existing package config errors.
Opened cloud PR for RelayFile observer integration entry: Opened cloud PR for RelayFile observer integration entry

raw.question: Opened cloud PR for RelayFile observer integration entry
raw.chosen: Opened cloud PR for RelayFile observer integration entry
raw.reasoning: Moved the observer route and integrations-page entry onto a clean branch from origin/main so the PR excludes stale deleted-branch commits and unrelated untracked workflow files.

events.content: Added Cloudflare Pages deploy placeholder for file observer: Added Cloudflare Pages deploy placeholder for file observer

raw.question: Added Cloudflare Pages deploy placeholder for file observer
raw.chosen: Added Cloudflare Pages deploy placeholder for file observer
raw.reasoning: Cloud should own deploying the published @relayfile/file-observer package; the production workflow now packs version 0.1.0 at deploy time so cloud npm ci does not depend on the package before deployment.

events.content: Keep file observer routing in cloud and publish only the observer app: Keep file observer routing in cloud and publish only the observer app

raw.question: Keep file observer routing in cloud and publish only the observer app
raw.chosen: Keep file observer routing in cloud and publish only the observer app
raw.reasoning: The relayfile file-observer-router package is a deployment-specific proxy for relayfile.dev; agentrelay.com path routing is cloud-owned, while @relayfile/file-observer should be the reusable published app package.

events.content: Deleted file-observer-router package: Deleted file-observer-router package

raw.question: Deleted file-observer-router package
raw.chosen: Deleted file-observer-router package
raw.reasoning: Routing for agentrelay.com/observer/file is owned by cloud, and the relayfile npm surface should publish only the reusable @relayfile/file-observer app.

events.content: Pack relayfile npm CLI with native binaries: Pack relayfile npm CLI with native binaries

raw.question: Pack relayfile npm CLI with native binaries
raw.chosen: Pack relayfile npm CLI with native binaries
raw.reasoning: The installed CLI must work even when npm postinstall scripts are disabled; packaging platform-specific cmd/relayfile-cli binaries lets scripts/run.js launch an included binary and avoids the missing bin/relayfile failure.

events.content: Implemented npm CLI binary packaging and verified packed global installs with npm scripts both disabled and enabled; leaving the pre-existing active trajectory open.
Added prepack hardening and confirmed the npm tarball contains platform binaries; temp global installs pass with ignore-scripts true and false.

retrospective.summary: Packaged relayfile npm CLI with native platform binaries, updated publish workflow to build and ship them, and verified packed global installs with npm scripts enabled and disabled.
